record.field: InstantDB is an open-source real-time backend that gives your frontend a client-side database which syncs transparently with the server.

InstantDB is an open-source real-time backend that gives your frontend a client-side database which syncs transparently with the server.

Often described as a modern Firebase, Instant combines auth, permissions, file storage, presence, and live queries into a single backend. You write relational queries in the shape of the data you want, and Instant handles fetching, permission checks, offline caching, optimistic updates, and rollbacks for you. Every query is multiplayer by default, making it well suited to collaborative apps like Notion or Figma.

Key features
  • Real-time sync engine: a client-side triple store that stays in sync with a server-side Postgres triple store
  • InstaQL relational query language (GraphQL-like) backed by a datalog query engine
  • Built-in authentication, fine-grained permissions (Google CEL), and file storage
  • Multiplayer by default with presence and ephemeral state (cursors, who's online)
  • Offline-first caching via IndexedDB on web and AsyncStorage on React Native
  • SDKs for JavaScript, React, and React Native; self-hostable with Docker Compose
Best for

Teams building real-time, collaborative, or local-first web and mobile apps who want a turnkey backend without wiring up auth, websockets, and a sync layer by hand. A strong fit for self-hosting a Firebase/Supabase-style alternative on your own infrastructure.

services: postgres: image: ghcr.io/instantdb/postgresql:postgresql-16-pg-hint-plan restart: unless-stopped environment: POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${POSTGRES_PASSWORD} POSTGRES_USER: {{ POSTGRES_USER }} POSTGRES_DB: {{ POSTGRES_DB }} volumes: - ./backend_db:/var/lib/postgresql/data healthcheck: test: - CMD - pg_isready - -U - {{ POSTGRES_USER }} interval: 10s timeout: 5s retries: 5 command: - postgres - -c - wal_level=logical - -c - max_replication_slots=4 - -c - max_wal_senders=4 - -c - shared_preload_libraries=pg_hint_plan - -c - random_page_cost=1.1 container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_postgres' networks: - internal server: depends_on: postgres: condition: service_healthy image: hari1367709/instantdb-server:latest restart: unless-stopped working_dir: /app environment: DATABASE_URL: postgresql://{{ POSTGRES_USER }}:${POSTGRES_PASSWORD}@postgres:5432/{{ POSTGRES_DB }} NREPL_BIND_ADDRESS: 0.0.0.0 PORT: '8888' HOST: 0.0.0.0 AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID: ${AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID} AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY: ${AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY} AWS_REGION: ${AWS_REGION:-us-east-1} PYTHONUNBUFFERED: '1' NODE_ENV: production command: - java - -Djava.awt.headless=true - -server - -jar - target/instant-standalone.jar ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:8888' logging: driver: json-file options: max-size: 10m max-file: '3' healthcheck: test: - CMD-SHELL - curl -f http://localhost:8888/health || exit 1 interval: 30s timeout: 10s retries: 3 start_period: 40s container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Dump consistente de PostgreSQL (servicio en marcha) → $BACKUP_DATA_DIR docker compose exec -T postgres sh -c 'pg_dump -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --no-owner -Fc' \ > "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump" echo "pg_dump OK ($(wc -c < "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump") bytes)"

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Levanta solo la DB, espera readiness y restaura docker compose up -d postgres for i in $(seq 1 30); do docker compose exec -T postgres sh -c 'pg_isready -U "$POSTGRES_USER"' && break sleep 2 done docker compose exec -T postgres sh -c 'pg_restore -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --clean --if-exists --no-owner' \ < "$RESTORE_DATA_DIR/db.dump" # Verificación: la DB no quedó vacía (sh -c con dobles comillas; $VAR del contenedor con \$) N=$(docker compose exec -T postgres sh -c "psql -U \$POSTGRES_USER -d \$POSTGRES_DB -tAc \"SELECT count(*) FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema='public'\"") echo "tablas restauradas: $N" test "$N" -gt 0
